author: Execs
text:
Resolve SPEC §16.13: new-side assertion, not old-bl fixture

Conformance test 13 asserts the new bl routes a state_url workspace to the tracker, never to its own git's worktree — not, as SPEC §16.13 literally said with '(fixture)', a pinned pre-spec binary run against state_url.

Audit verdict: the new-side assertion is not merely an adequate stand-in — an old-binary fixture would be the wrong shape. The §12 caveat is explicitly 'documented, not engineered against'; an old binary's behaviour is immutable and outside this codebase, so a fixture gates nothing this repo can regress. Being invariant it would pass against pre-spec code and so could never fail-then-pass across the refactor, as the §16 preamble requires of every conformance test.

Reword SPEC §16.13 to describe the realized, gating-coherent test so spec and suite agree, and bake the rationale into the t13 doc comment. Comment/prose only — no behaviour change.
